[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 钦定四库全书 - Official designation of the Siku Quan Shu, the largest collection of books in Chinese history, compiled during the Qing Dynasty.
  • 子部十二 - Classification of the Siku Quan Shu, which includes works in the 'Zi' (miscellaneous) category, divided into twelve subcategories.
  • 提要 - Summary or preface, often found in classical Chinese texts to provide an overview of the content.
  • 中朝故事 - Title referring to a collection of historical anecdotes or stories from the 'Central Court,' likely referring to the Tang Dynasty.
  • 杂事之属 - Subcategory of 'Xiaoshuo' that deals with miscellaneous or anecdotal events.
  • 南唐尉迟偓撰 - Indicating that the work was written by Wei Chi Wo during the Southern Tang Dynasty.
  • 偓履贯未详 - Meaning 'Wei Chi Wo's background is not detailed,' suggesting that the author's personal history is unknown.
  • 奉防纂进 - Meaning 'compiled and presented upon imperial command.'
  • 李氏有国时 - Referring to the time when the Li family ruled, likely the Li family of the Southern Tang Dynasty.
  • 为史官承命所作 - Meaning 'as a historian commissioned to write it.'
  • 自以为出太宗之后 - Meaning 'claimed to be descended from Emperor Taizong,' likely referring to the Tang Dynasty's Emperor Taizong.
  • 承唐统绪 - Meaning 'to inherit the Tang Dynasty's lineage.'
  • 称长安为中朝也 - Meaning 'referring to Chang'an as the Central Court,' likely a reference to the Tang Dynasty's capital.
  • 杂録神异怪幻之事 - Meaning 'a miscellaneous collection of supernatural and strange events.'
  • 不可尽据者 - Meaning 'not all of which can be fully relied upon.'
  • 司马光通鉴考异 - Referring to the 'Zizhi Tongjian' (Comprehensive Mirror to Aid in Government) and its 'Kao Yi' (Distinguishing Differences) section, a historical work by Sima Guang.
  • 路岩倾害刘瞻 - Referring to the historical event where Lu Yan, a Tang official, plotted against Liu Zhan.
  • 赖幽州节度使张公素上疏申理 - Meaning 'relying on Zhang Gong Su, the governor of Youzhou, who submitted a petition to clear the matter.'
  • 考是时镇幽州者乃张允伸非张公素 - Meaning 'investigating at that time, the governor of Youzhou was Zhang Yun Shen, not Zhang Gong Su.'
  • 郑畋胎 - Referring to a story involving Zheng Tian, a Tang official, and a ghost pregnancy.
  • 与唐人所作齐推女首尾全同 - Meaning 'completely identical to the beginning and end of the story 'Qi Tui Nu Chuan' written by a Tang person.'
  • 司马光考异虽摘其以彦昭代凝领盐铁之误 - Meaning 'Sima Guang's 'Kao Yi' section pointed out the error of replacing Yan Zhao with Ning in the salt and iron administration.'
  • 昭宗菩萨蛮词 - Referring to a poem by Emperor Zhaozong of Tang, titled 'Pusa Man.'
  • 选家所录 - Meaning 'collected by literary critics.'
  • 茍与正史分别防观 - Meaning 'if one distinguishes and observes separately from the official history.'
  • 去讹存是 - Meaning 'to discard errors and retain truths.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 钦定四库全书 - The Siku Quan Shu, a massive compilation of Chinese texts ordered by the Qing Dynasty.
  • 南唐尉迟偓 - Wei Chi Wo, a historian from the Southern Tang Dynasty.
  • 李氏有国时 - The Southern Tang Dynasty, ruled by the Li family, which was a successor state to the Tang Dynasty.
  • 长安为中朝 - Chang'an, the capital of the Tang Dynasty, referred to as the 'Central Court.'
  • 司马光通鉴考异 - Sima Guang's 'Zizhi Tongjian' and its 'Kao Yi' section, which critically examines historical sources.
  • 路岩倾害刘瞻 - A historical event involving political intrigue and betrayal during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 郑畋胎 - A supernatural story involving a Tang official and a ghost pregnancy.
  • 昭宗菩萨蛮词 - A poem by Emperor Zhaozong of Tang, a key figure in the late Tang Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 钦定四库全书 - The Siku Quan Shu.
  • 子部十二 - The 'Zi' category in the Siku Quan Shu.
  • 中朝故事 - 'Zhongzhao Gushi,' a collection of historical anecdotes.
  • 杂事之属 - A subcategory of 'Xiaoshuo.'
  • 南唐尉迟偓 - Wei Chi Wo, a historian from the Southern Tang Dynasty.
  • 司马光 - Sima Guang, a prominent historian and statesman of the Song Dynasty.
  • 路岩 - Lu Yan, a Tang Dynasty official.
  • 刘瞻 - Liu Zhan, a Tang Dynasty official.
  • 张公素 - Zhang Gong Su, a Tang Dynasty official.
  • 张允伸 - Zhang Yun Shen, a Tang Dynasty official.
  • 郑畋 - Zheng Tian, a Tang Dynasty official.
  • 昭宗 - Emperor Zhaozong of Tang.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 臣 - A formal term used by officials when addressing superiors, indicating a respectful and subservient tone.
  • 谨案 - A formal expression meaning 'carefully recorded' or 'meticulously noted,' often used in official documents.
  • 自序 - A self-introduction or preface written by the author.
  • 庙号 - The temple name given to an emperor after his death, used in official records.
  • 纪年 - A system of dating used in historical records, often based on the reign of an emperor.
  • 诸子辨 - A work or discussion on the various schools of thought in ancient China.
  • 诸说纷纭 - A phrase meaning 'various opinions are mixed,' indicating disagreement or uncertainty.
  • 梗概 - A brief summary or outline of the main points.
  • 具官称臣 - A formal way of introducing oneself, indicating one's official title and status.
  • 不可考 - A phrase meaning 'cannot be verified' or 'uncertain,' often used when historical records are unclear.
  • 资治通鉴 - A monumental historical work compiled by Sima Guang during the Song Dynasty, covering Chinese history from the Warring States period to the Five Dynasties.
  • 司马光 - A prominent historian and statesman of the Song Dynasty, known for compiling the *Zizhi Tongjian*.
  • 纪 - A chapter or section in a book, often used in historical works.
  • 袭 - A term meaning 'to inherit' or 'to take over,' often used in the context of military or political succession.
  • 藩镇 - A regional military governorate during the Tang Dynasty, often with significant autonomy.
  • 强弱 - A phrase meaning 'strength and weakness,' used to describe the balance of power.
  • 吟咏 - A term meaning 'recitation' or 'singing,' often used in the context of poetry.
  • 神竒 - A term meaning 'miraculous' or 'supernatural,' often used to describe extraordinary events.
  • 正史 - The official historical records, often referring to the *Twenty-Four Histories*.
  • 防证 - A term meaning 'complementary evidence' or 'verification,' used to describe how different sources can support each other.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金华子 - A pseudonym or pen name used by the author, Duan Chongyuan, indicating his admiration for the legendary figure Huang Chuping.
  • 刘崇逺 - The author of the text, a scholar and official during the Southern Tang Dynasty.
  • 南唐 - A state that existed during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period, ruled by the Li family.
  • 李昪 - The founding emperor of the Southern Tang Dynasty, posthumously known as Emperor Liezu.
  • 李景 - The second emperor of the Southern Tang Dynasty, posthumously known as Emperor Wenzong.
  • 升元 - The era name used by Emperor Liezu, indicating the year of his ascension to the throne.
  • 大中 - An era name used during the Tang Dynasty, indicating a period of relative stability and prosperity.
  • 晁公武 - A scholar and bibliographer of the Song Dynasty, known for his *Zhiyuan Lu* (Record of the Zhiyuan).
  • 陈振孙 - A scholar and bibliographer of the Song Dynasty, known for his *Zi Shu Lu* (Catalogue of Books).
  • 宋濂 - A scholar and historian of the Ming Dynasty, known for his *Wen Yen Chao* (Essays on Literature).
  • 资治通鉴 - A monumental historical work compiled by Sima Guang during the Song Dynasty, covering Chinese history from the Warring States period to the Five Dynasties.
  • 司马光 - A prominent historian and statesman of the Song Dynasty, known for compiling the *Zizhi Tongjian*.
  • 令狐绹 - A prominent official during the Tang Dynasty, known for his political acumen.
  • 李景让 - A prominent official during the Tang Dynasty, known for his integrity and wisdom.
  • 王师范 - A prominent official during the Tang Dynasty, known for his administrative skills.
  • 王式 - A prominent official during the Tang Dynasty, known for his military leadership.
  • 刘鄩 - A prominent general during the Tang Dynasty, known for his military campaigns.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '臣等谨案金华子二卷南唐刘崇逺撰' introduces the author and the work, but the structure is somewhat compressed. It can be expanded to: 'We, the officials, carefully record that the *Jinhua Zi* in two volumes was written by Liu Chongyuan of the Southern Tang.'
  • The sentence '崇逺家本河南唐末避黄巢之乱渡江南徙仕李氏为文林郎大理司直' describes Liu Chongyuan's background and career. It can be expanded to: 'Liu Chongyuan, originally from Henan, fled the chaos caused by Huang Chao at the end of the Tang Dynasty and moved south. He served the Li family, holding the titles of Wenlin Lang and Dali Sizhi.'
  • The sentence '尝慕皇初平之为人自号金华子因以为所著书名' explains why Liu Chongyuan chose the pen name *Jinhua Zi*. It can be expanded to: 'He admired the character of Huang Chuping and thus gave himself the pen name *Jinhua Zi*, using it as the title for his writings.'
  • The sentence '崇逺有自序一篇颇具梗概序末题名具官称臣不着年月' describes the author's preface and its contents. It can be expanded to: 'Liu Chongyuan wrote a self-introduction that provides a brief summary. At the end of the preface, he introduces himself with his official title, but does not specify the date.'
  • The sentence '其书宋艺文志作三卷世无本惟散见永乐大典者搜采尚得六十余条' discusses the current state of the text. It can be expanded to: 'The book is listed as three volumes in the *Song Yiwenzhi* (Catalogue of Arts and Literature), but no complete copies have survived. Only fragments can be found in the *Yongle Dadian* (Great Canon of the Yongle Era), from which about sixty entries have been collected.'
  • The sentence '核其所记皆唐末朝野之故事与晁氏所云录唐大中后事者相合' summarizes the content of the book. It can be expanded to: 'Upon examination, the content of the book records stories from the late Tang period, aligning with what Chao Gongwu said about recording events after the *Dazhong* era.'
  • The sentence '其中于将相之贤否藩镇之强弱以及文章吟咏神竒鬼怪之事靡所不载多足与正史相防证' describes the range of topics covered in the book. It can be expanded to: 'The book covers a wide range of topics, including the virtues and vices of generals and ministers, the strengths and weaknesses of the *fan zhen* (regional military governors), as well as literary works, songs, and stories of supernatural phenomena. These provide valuable complementary evidence to the official histories.'
  • The sentence '观资治通鉴所载宣宗引对令狐绹李景让禀母训王师范拜县令王式驭乱卒诸事皆本是书则司马光亦极取之' discusses the influence of the book on later historical works. It can be expanded to: 'The *Zizhi Tongjian* records events such as Emperor Xuanzong's audience with Linghu Tong, Li Jingrang's respect for his mother's teachings, Wang Shifan's appointment as county magistrate, and Wang Shi's control of chaos, all of which are based on this book. Thus, Sima Guang also highly valued it.'
  • The sentence '惟其纪刘鄩袭兖州一条以兖帅' mentions a specific entry in the book. It can be expanded to: 'However, the entry about Liu Xun inheriting the governorship of Yan Prefecture is a bit ambiguous, as it only mentions the governor of Yan.'

Proper Nouns

  • 金华子 - The pen name of Liu Chongyuan.
  • 刘崇逺 - The author of the text.
  • 南唐 - The state ruled by the Li family during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.
  • 李昪 - The founding emperor of the Southern Tang Dynasty, posthumously known as Emperor Liezu.
  • 李景 - The second emperor of the Southern Tang Dynasty, posthumously known as Emperor Wenzong.
  • 升元 - The era name used by Emperor Liezu.
  • 大中 - An era name used during the Tang Dynasty.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 為張姓而考之五代歐薛二史 - To investigate the surname Zhang by examining the histories of the Five Dynasties by Ouyang Xiu and Xue Juzheng. Indicates a scholarly approach to verifying historical records.
  • 要其大致可信者多 - In essence, most of what is generally credible is reliable. Indicates that despite variations, the core content is trustworthy.
  • 胡應麟九流緒論乃以鄙淺譏之 - Hu Yinglin, in his *Nine Streams Postscript*, criticized it for being shallow and vulgar. Refers to a specific criticism by Hu Yinglin.
  • 明人詭薄好為大言以售欺不足信也 - The people of the Ming dynasty were deceitful and fond of making grand statements to deceive, which is not trustworthy. Criticizes the Ming scholars' tendency to exaggerate.
  • 乾隆四十六年九月恭校上 - In the ninth month of the forty-sixth year of the Qianlong era, respectfully proofread and presented. Provides the date and context of the proofreading.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 五代歐薛二史 - Refers to the histories of the Five Dynasties compiled by Ouyang Xiu and Xue Juzheng.
  • 胡應麟 - A scholar from the late Ming dynasty known for his critical writings.
  • 九流緒論 - A work by Hu Yinglin.
  • 劉基郁離子 - A work by Liu Ji, a famous statesman and writer from the early Ming dynasty.
  • 蘇伯衡空同子 - A work by Su Boyan, a scholar from the late Yuan and early Ming dynasties.
  • 明人 - Refers to scholars and writers from the Ming dynasty.
  • 乾隆四十六年 - The forty-sixth year of the Qianlong era (1781 AD).
  • 紀昀 - A prominent scholar and official during the Qing dynasty, known for his role in the compilation of the *Siku Quanshu*.
  • 陸錫熊 - Another scholar and official during the Qing dynasty.
  • 孫士毅 - A scholar and official during the Qing dynasty.
  • 陸費墀 - A scholar and official during the Qing dynasty, known for his work in proofreading and editing.

Proper Nouns

  • 張姓 - The surname Zhang.
  • 五代 - The Five Dynasties period.
  • 歐薛二史 - The histories compiled by Ouyang Xiu and Xue Juzheng.
  • 兗帥 - The governor of Yan.
  • 葛從周 - Ge Congzhou, a historical figure.
  • 胡應麟 - Hu Yinglin, a scholar from the late Ming dynasty.
  • 九流緒論 - *Nine Streams Postscript* by Hu Yinglin.
  • 劉基郁離子 - *Yu Zi* by Liu Ji.
  • 蘇伯衡空同子 - *Kongtong Zi* by Su Boyan.
  • 明人 - Scholars and writers from the Ming dynasty.
  • 乾隆四十六年 - The forty-sixth year of the Qianlong era (1781 AD).
  • 紀昀 - Ji Yun, a prominent scholar and official during the Qing dynasty.
  • 陸錫熊 - Lu Xixiong, a scholar and official during the Qing dynasty.
  • 孫士毅 - Sun Shiyi, a scholar and official during the Qing dynasty.
  • 陸費墀 - Lu Fei Chi, a scholar and official during the Qing dynasty.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 欲往谒耳 - Wants to go and pay respects; indicates the person's intention to seek an audience with someone in power.
  • 旧识耶 - Is he an old acquaintance? Question asking whether the person being addressed knows the individual they are referring to.
  • 闻杜琮相公出镇淮南 - Heard that Du Cong, the chancellor, is being dispatched to govern Huainan; indicates the reason for the person's visit.
  • 非旧识 - Not an old acquaintance; indicates that the person does not know Du Cong personally.
  • 但留邸中伺候 - Just stay in the lodging and wait; instructs the person to remain in the provided accommodation until further notice.
  • 终身获厚禄焉 - Will receive a generous salary for life; indicates a promise of lifelong financial security.
  • 即宪皇少子也 - Is the youngest son of Emperor Xianzong; provides information about the identity of the emperor being referred to.
  • 皇昆即穆宗也 - The eldest brother is Emperor Muzong; indicates the relationship between the two emperors.
  • 深忌焉 - Deeply feared him; indicates the emperor's fear of someone else.
  • 适有防王可下马 - Just saw a prince who can dismount; indicates a specific event that occurred.
  • 落马已不救矣 - Has fallen off his horse and cannot be saved; indicates a tragic outcome.
  • 游行江表间 - Wandered in the southern regions; indicates the person's movements after becoming a monk.
  • 中人请还京遂即位 - The eunuchs requested his return to the capital, and he ascended the throne; indicates a series of events leading to the person becoming emperor.
  • 僖宗皇帝以咸通三年降诞 - Emperor Xizong was born in the third year of the Xiantong era; provides the birth details of the emperor.
  • 每入对敭皆自备两牙盘果食 - Each time he entered to present his views, he would prepare two trays of fruits and snacks; indicates a specific ritual or custom.
  • 不敢侍从时令孜见陈敬瑄为西川节度 - He did not dare to accompany him when Lingzi saw Chen Jingxuan as the governor of Xichuan; indicates a specific situation where the person was absent.
  • 求为监军而殂 - Sought to become a military supervisor and died; indicates the person's final wish and fate.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 赵淮南人也 - Refers to Zhao, a person from Huainan; indicates his origin.
  • 杜琮相公 - Refers to Du Cong, a high-ranking official and chancellor.
  • 淮南 - Refers to the Huainan region; a significant area in Chinese history.
  • 中官仇士良 - Refers to Cao Shiliang, a eunuch and influential figure during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 禁苑间 - Refers to the imperial gardens; a place where the emperor and his entourage would often visit.
  • 武宗 - Refers to Emperor Wuzong; the emperor who reigned during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 文宗 - Refers to Emperor Wenzong; the emperor who preceded Wuzong.
  • 武宗初登极 - Refers to the early reign of Emperor Wuzong; indicates a specific period in history.
  • 深忌焉 - Indicates the emperor's fear of someone else; likely referring to the eunuchs or other political figures.
  • 禁苑 - Refers to the imperial gardens; a place where the emperor and his entourage would often visit.
  • 中人 - Refers to eunuchs; who played a significant role in the imperial court.
  • 僖宗 - Refers to Emperor Xizong; the emperor who reigned during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 咸通三年 - Refers to the third year of the Xiantong era; a specific period in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 田令孜 - Refers to a eunuch and influential figure during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 阿父 - A term of endearment used by the emperor to address a close advisor or relative.
  • 朝纲由已 - The court rules by himself; indicates the emperor's control over the government.
  • 翠华还京 - Refers to the return of the imperial procession to the capital; a significant event in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 陈敬瑄 - Refers to Chen Jingxuan; a military governor during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 监军 - Refers to a military supervisor; a position of significant authority.

Proper Nouns

  • 赵 - Refers to Zhao; a person from Huainan.
  • 杜琮 - Refers to Du Cong; a high-ranking official and chancellor.
  • 淮南 - Refers to the Huainan region.
  • 中官仇士良 - Refers to Cao Shiliang; a eunuch and influential figure during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 禁苑 - Refers to the imperial gardens.
  • 武宗 - Refers to Emperor Wuzong.
  • 文宗 - Refers to Emperor Wenzong.
  • 武宗初登极 - Refers to the early reign of Emperor Wuzong.
  • 禁苑 - Refers to the imperial gardens.
  • 中人 - Refers to eunuchs.
  • 僖宗 - Refers to Emperor Xizong.
  • 咸通三年 - Refers to the third year of the Xiantong era.
  • 田令孜 - Refers to a eunuch and influential figure during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 阿父 - A term of endearment used by the emperor to address a close advisor or relative.
  • 朝纲由已 - Refers to the emperor's control over the government.
  • 翠华还京 - Refers to the return of the imperial procession to the capital.
  • 陈敬瑄 - Refers to Chen Jingxuan; a military governor during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 监军 - Refers to a military supervisor.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 昭宗皇帝即僖皇弟也 - Introduces Emperor Zhaozong as the younger brother of Emperor Xizong, a common way to establish lineage in historical narratives.
  • 咸通八年丁亥嵗降生 - Indicates the birth year of Emperor Zhaozong in the traditional Chinese calendar, using the stem-branch system.
  • 文德元年三月即位 - Specifies the date of Emperor Zhaozong's ascension to the throne, using the era name 'Wende'.
  • 体貌端明 - Describes Emperor Zhaozong's dignified and clear appearance, a common way to praise a ruler's physical presence.
  • 人望伟如也 - Means that Emperor Zhaozong was highly respected and admired, emphasizing his reputation.
  • 运钟艰险 - Indicates that Emperor Zhaozong's reign was marked by difficult and perilous times.
  • 智量过人 - Describes Emperor Zhaozong as exceptionally intelligent and capable.
  • 每与侍臣言论商较时政曾无厌倦 - Means that Emperor Zhaozong frequently discussed and debated state affairs with his ministers without tiring.
  • 干宁三年凤翔李茂贞与朝臣有隙 - Indicates that in the third year of the Ganning era, Li Maozhen of Fengxiang had a conflict with the court officials.
  • 将欲搆难犯干神京 - Means that Li Maozhen was planning to cause trouble and threaten the capital.
  • 上乃顺动欲幸太原 - Indicates that Emperor Zhaozong decided to move to Taiyuan to avoid the conflict.
  • 行止渭北华州韩建迎归郡中 - Means that when Emperor Zhaozong stopped in Huazhou, Han Jian welcomed him back to the city.
  • 上郁郁不乐 - Describes Emperor Zhaozong as feeling depressed and unhappy.
  • 时登城西齐云眺望 - Indicates that Emperor Zhaozong often climbed to the western part of the city to gaze at the clouds.
  • 登楼遥望秦宫殿 - Refers to the palaces of Qin, a reference to the ancient state of Qin.
  • 茫茫只见双飞燕 - Describes the scene as vast and only showing two flying swallows.
  • 渭水一条流千山与万丘 - Means that the Wei River flows through countless mountains and hills.
  • 是英雄迎孥归故宫 - Means that the heroes welcomed their families back to the old palace.
  • 又一曰飘飖且在三峯下 - Refers to the Three Peaks, a common geographical reference.
  • 秋风往往堪沾洒 - Means that the autumn wind often brought tears.
  • 肠断忆仙宫 - Describes the feeling of heartbreak and longing for the fairy palace.
  • 朦胧烟雾中思 - Means that in the hazy smoke and mist, he thought deeply.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 昭宗皇帝即僖皇弟也 - Emperor Zhaozong was the younger brother of Emperor Xizong, both of whom were emperors of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 咸通八年丁亥嵗降生 - The year 867 in the traditional Chinese calendar, using the stem-branch system.
  • 文德元年三月即位 - The year 888 in the traditional Chinese calendar, using the era name 'Wende'.
  • 体貌端明 - A common way to describe a ruler's dignified and clear appearance.
  • 人望伟如也 - A common way to praise a ruler's reputation.
  • 运钟艰险 - Refers to the difficult and perilous times during Emperor Zhaozong's reign.
  • 智量过人 - A common way to describe a ruler's exceptional intelligence and capability.
  • 每与侍臣言论商较时政曾无厌倦 - A common way to describe a ruler's active involvement in state affairs.
  • 干宁三年凤翔李茂贞与朝臣有隙 - Li Maozhen of Fengxiang had a conflict with the court officials in the third year of the Ganning era.
  • 将欲搆难犯干神京 - Li Maozhen was planning to cause trouble and threaten the capital.
  • 上乃顺动欲幸太原 - Emperor Zhaozong decided to move to Taiyuan to avoid the conflict.
  • 行止渭北华州韩建迎归郡中 - When Emperor Zhaozong stopped in Huazhou, Han Jian welcomed him back to the city.
  • 上郁郁不乐 - A common way to describe a ruler's depression and unhappiness.
  • 时登城西齐云眺望 - Emperor Zhaozong often climbed to the western part of the city to gaze at the clouds.
  • 登楼遥望秦宫殿 - Refers to the palaces of Qin, a reference to the ancient state of Qin.
  • 茫茫只见双飞燕 - A common way to describe a vast and desolate scene.
